channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: [AINews] How to land a job at a frontier lab (on Pretraining)
item.description: a quiet day before google i/o lets us amplify a notable blogpost

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Haiku 4.5
item.description: May 19, 07:00 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Tencent Tests 'Pao Zi AI' Knowledge Base Tool for WeChat Article Collections
item.description: Tencent is internally testing Pao Zi AI, a knowledge base tool that imports WeChat saved articles into a searchable, AI-queryable personal repository.

item.title: AgiBot-Linked Dexterous Hand Startup Reaches Unicorn Status in Just 4 Months
item.description: AgiBot spin-off Lingjiandian becomes China's fastest unicorn in humanoid robot components, raising hundreds of millions yuan just 4 months after founding.

item.title: CXMT Files for STAR Market IPO: Behind the Guangdong Capital Supporting China's Top Memory Chip Maker
item.description: China's leading memory chip maker CXMT files for STAR Market IPO with Q1 revenue up 700% YoY, backed by state funds and Guangdong investors holding ~5%.

item.title: WeChat Reading AI Skill Goes Viral, Letting Users Query Their Personal Reading History
item.description: WeChat Reading launches an AI Skill that accesses personal reading data — from bookshelf browsing to note exports — going viral on Chinese social media.

item.title: XPeng Robotaxi First Production Vehicle Rolls Off Line, Pilot Operations Expected in H2 2026
item.description: China's first full-stack self-developed robotaxi from a complete vehicle maker enters mass production, with 3,000 TOPS on-board computing and L4 capability.

item.title: Critical Vulnerability Exposes Industrial Robot Fleets to Hacking
item.description: The vulnerability, CVE-2026-8153, affects Universal Robots PolyScope 5 and it can be exploited for OS command injection. The post Critical Vulnerability Exposes Industrial Robot Fleets to Hacking appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Popular GitHub Action Tags Redirected to Imposter Commit to Steal CI/CD Credentials
item.description: In yet another software supply chain attack, threat actors have compromised the popular GitHub Actions workflow, actions-cool/issues-helper, to run malicious code that harvests sensitive credentials and exfiltrates them to an attacker-controlled server. "Every existing tag in the repository has...

item.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Pushes Malicious AntV npm Packages via Compromised Maintainer Account
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a fresh software supply chain attack campaign that has compromised various npm packages associated with the @antv ecosystem as part of the ongoing Mini Shai-Hulud attack wave. "The attack affects packages tied to the npm maintainer account atool, including...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude.ai and Claude Code login
item.description: May 19, 04:51 UTCResolved - This issue has been resolved.May 19, 04:46 UTCMonitoring - From 04:34–04:42 UTC, we saw elevated errors and latency on requests within the Claude.ai applications. Additionally, users may have experienced errors when attempting to login, including authenticating Claude...

item.title: Solvita: Enhancing Large Language Models for Competitive Programming via Agentic Evolution
item.description: arXiv:2605.15301v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) still struggle with the rigorous reasoning demands of hard competitive programming. While recent multi-agent frameworks attempt to bridge this reliability gap, they remain fundamentally stateless: they rely on static...

item.title: SMCEvolve: Principled Scientific Discovery via Sequential Monte Carlo Evolution
item.description: arXiv:2605.15308v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-driven program evolution has emerged as a powerful tool for automated scientific discovery, yet existing frameworks offer no principled guide for designing their individual components and provide no guarantee that the search converges. We...

item.title: Context Pruning for Coding Agents via Multi-Rubric Latent Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2605.15315v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-powered coding agents spend the majority of their token budget reading repository files, yet much of the retrieved code is irrelevant to the task at hand. Existing learned pruners compress this context with a single-objective sequence labeler,...

item.title: Zero-Shot Goal Recognition with Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.15333v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models have recently reached near-parity with classical planners on well-known planning domains, yet this competence relies on world-knowledge exploitation rather than genuine symbolic reasoning. Goal recognition is a complementary...

item.title: Belief Engine: Configurable and Inspectable Stance Dynamics in Multi-Agent LLM Deliberation
item.description: arXiv:2605.15343v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-based agents are increasingly used to simulate deliberative interactions such as negotiation, conflict resolution, and multi-turn opinion exchange. Yet generated transcripts often do not reveal why an agent's stance changes: movement may reflect...

item.title: Systematic Optimization of Real-Time Diffusion Model Inference on Apple M3 Ultra
item.description: arXiv:2605.16259v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While real-time image generation using diffusion models has advanced rapidly on NVIDIA GPUs, systematic optimization research on non-CUDA platforms such as Apple Silicon remains extremely limited. In this study, we conducted comprehensive optimization...

item.title: Mirror Descent-Type Algorithms for the Variational Inequality Problem with Functional Constraints
item.description: arXiv:2605.16262v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Variational inequalities play a key role in machine learning research, such as generative adversarial networks, reinforcement learning, adversarial training, and generative models. This paper is devoted to the constrained variational inequality...

item.title: Reducing Credit Assignment Variance via Counterfactual Reasoning Paths
item.description: arXiv:2605.16302v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement learning for multi-step reasoning with large language models (LLMs) often relies on sparse terminal rewards, leading to poor credit assignment conditions where the final feedback is evenly propagated across all intermediate decisions....

item.title: SignMuon: Communication-Efficient Distributed Muon Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2605.16311v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Distributed training of large neural networks is bottlenecked by full-precision gradient communication and by coordinatewise optimizers that ignore the matrix structure of weight tensors. We propose Sign-Muon, a 1-bit, matrix-aware optimizer that...

item.title: When Actions Disappear: Adversarial Action Removal in Self-Play Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2605.16312v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study adversarial action masking in self-play reinforcement learning: an attacker selectively removes legal actions from a victim's action set. Unlike observation or action perturbations, removal eliminates decision options before the agent acts....

item.title: The Scaling Laws of Skills in LLM Agent Systems
item.description: arXiv:2605.16508v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As agent systems scale, skills accumulate into large reusable libraries, yet their scaling laws remain poorly understood. Across 15 frontier LLMs, 1,141 real-world skills, and over 3M routing or execution decisions, we identify two coupled laws....

item.title: PQR: A Framework to Generate Diverse and Realistic User Queries that Elicit QA Agent Failures
item.description: arXiv:2605.16551v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Evaluating LLM-based agents remains challenging because identifying meaningful failure cases often requires substantial human effort to design realistic test scenarios. Prior works primarily focus on automatically discovering agent failures induced by...

item.title: Scaling Accessible Mathematics on arXiv: HTML Conversion and MathML 4
item.description: arXiv:2605.16562v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We report on the ongoing development of arXiv's HTML Papers offering, available on every new TeX/LaTeX submission since its initial release in 2023. The main highlights from 2025 and early 2026 are: (i) community-driven improvements to HTML fidelity...

item.title: Beyond Sentiment Classification: A Generative Framework for Emotion Intensity Evaluation in Text
item.description: arXiv:2605.16613v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce a novel approach to emotion modeling that shifts the focus from identification to evaluation, addressing the limitations of discrete classification in applied domains such as finance. By constructing a dataset of emotional intensity...

item.title: SKG-Eval: Stateful Evaluation of Multi-Turn Dialogue via Incremental Semantic Knowledge Graphs
item.description: arXiv:2605.16650v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Evaluating multi-turn dialogue systems remains challenging because response quality depends not only on the current prompt, but also on previously established entities, claims, and conversational commitments. Existing automatic evaluators, including...

item.title: Here’s why Elon Musk lost his suit against OpenAI
item.description: On Monday, the jury in Musk v. Altman dealt Elon Musk a major blow—reaching a unanimous advisory verdict that he had sued OpenAI too late and, as a result, his claims are barred by the applicable statutes of limitations. US District Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers immediately accepted it.  Musk...

item.title: Elevated errors for Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: May 18, 22:19 UTCResolved - The issue affecting Claude services has been resolved. Impact occurred from 21:47 to 22:18 UTC.May 18, 22:10 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating elevated errors on requests to Claude Opus 4.7. We will provide an update as soon as possible.

item.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
item.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public...

item.title: What to expect from Google this week
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. When Google opens its doors tomorrow for its annual developer conference, I/O, it will do so as a clear third place in the foundation model race. A year ago, at...

item.title: INTERPOL Operation Ramz Disrupts MENA Cybercrime Networks with 201 Arrests
item.description: INTERPOL has coordinated a first-of-its-kind cybercrime crackdown across the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) that led to 201 arrests and the identification of an additional 382 suspects. The initiative involved the efforts of 13 countries from the region between October 2025 and February 2026,...

item.title: The Signals That Matter – MIT Insider’s Panel
Inside Anduril and Meta’s quest to make smart glasses for warfare
item.description: The defense-tech company Anduril has shared new details about the augmented-reality headset for the military it’s prototyping with Meta, including a vision for ordering drone strikes via eye-tracking and voice commands. Quay Barnett, who leads the efforts as a vice president at Anduril following a...

item.title: Fine-Tuning NVIDIA Cosmos Predict 2.5 with LoRA/DoRA for Robot Video Generation
PaddleOCR 3.5: Running OCR and Document Parsing Tasks with a Transformers Backend
The Open Agent Leaderboard
Remote preview failing on Workers with attached Durable Object
item.description: May 18, 14:00 UTCResolved - Cloudflare identified an issue affecting the remote preview feature between 15:00 and 19:46 UTC. Workers configured with both a remote binding and a Durable Object failed to launch, throwing error code 10375.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Exchange 0-Day, npm Worm, Fake AI Repo, Cisco Exploit and More
item.description: Monday opens with a trust problem. A mail server flaw is under active use. A network control system was targeted. Trusted packages were poisoned. A fake model page pushed a stealer. Then came the familiar ransom claim: the data was returned and deleted. The pattern is clear. One weak dependency can...

item.title: The Next War Is Already Here. The West Isn't Ready. — Yaroslav Azhnyuk, The Fourth Law & Guest Host Noah Smith, Noahpinion
item.description: Ukrainian drone founder Yaroslav Azhnyuk went from pet cameras to AI-guided weapons. He and guest host Noah Smith make the case that the West is asleep at the wheel.

item.title: How to Reduce Phishing Exposure Before It Turns into Business Disruption
item.description: What happens when a phishing email looks clean enough to pass through security, but dangerous enough to expose the business after one click? That is the gap many SOCs still struggle with: the attacks that leave teams unsure what was exposed, who else was targeted, and how far the risk has spread....

item.title: Millions Impacted Across Several US Healthcare Data Breaches
item.description: Several healthcare data breaches impacting hundreds of thousands and even millions were added to the HHS tracker. The post Millions Impacted Across Several US Healthcare Data Breaches appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: ‘Claw Chain’ OpenClaw Flaws Allow Sandbox Escape, Backdoor Delivery
item.description: Four vulnerabilities in OpenClaw can be chained together to steal credentials, escape the sandbox, and plant persistent backdoors. The post ‘Claw Chain’ OpenClaw Flaws Allow Sandbox Escape, Backdoor Delivery appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: Musk v. Altman week 3, and Trump’s tech trading
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Musk v. Altman week 3: Musk and Altman traded blows over each other’s credibility. Now the jury will pick a side. In the final week of the Musk v....

item.title: 7-Eleven Data Breach Confirmed After ShinyHunters Ransom Demand
item.description: The hackers claimed to have stolen more than 600,000 Salesforce records, including personal information and corporate data. The post 7-Eleven Data Breach Confirmed After ShinyHunters Ransom Demand appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Researcher Drops MiniPlasma Windows Exploit for Unpatched 2020 CVE
item.description: The researcher dropped the MiniPlasma exploit that uses the original proof-of-concept (PoC) code targeting the bug. The post Researcher Drops MiniPlasma Windows Exploit for Unpatched 2020 CVE appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Haiku 4.5
item.description: May 18, 07:28 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.May 18, 06:12 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Issues with Cloudflare Log Explorer
item.description: May 18, 06:33 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 18, 05:57 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.May 18, 05:37 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is aware of and investigating an issue with Cloudflare Log Explorer. Affected customers may see delays...

item.title: PayPal Billing Issues
item.description: May 16, 19:13 UTCIdentified - The cause of this issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.May 16, 17:54 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is aware of and investigating issues paying for products with PayPal. Customers may experience an error when attempting to purchase or renew a product...

item.title: Elevated error rates on requests to multiple models
item.description: May 16, 18:24 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 16, 18:18 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.May 16, 18:08 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Latest open artifacts (#21): Open model bonanza! Gemma 4, DeepSeek V4, Kimi K2.6, MiMo 2.5, GLM-5.1 & others. On CAISI's V4 assessment.
item.description: An eventful month with one flagship release after another
